decisions.title: Use JWT for stateless API authentication
decisions.description: This decision was made after evaluating session-based authentication, API keys, and token-based approaches. JWT was chosen because it enables stateless authentication which is critical for horizontal scaling across multiple server instances. The team considered the trade-off of larger token sizes vs the elimination of server-side session storage. Implementation uses RS256 signing with 15-minute access tokens and 7-day refresh tokens stored in HTTP-only cookies.
decisions.explanation: Relevant to the current task context based on auth domain match and direct affect on the requesting agent's responsibilities. Previous compile requests from similar agents consistently ranked this decision highly.
